description: AI CPA Firm agent package (MagCPA test case): AGORZ Time & Billing summaries feed the CPA Firm Operations KPI Framework v1 (per-staff utilization/realization, per-location WIP aging, billing cycle, revenue per staff, client retention, CPE compliance rate — the last fed from the kernel's person-level compliance summary). Client return summaries feed the Client Tax Risk Indicators framework, benchmarked against IRS corporate tax ratios by industry segment (AI Analytical Source pattern; targets live in tenant benchmarks, not this manifest). Includes the Tax Preparation & Compliance Agent with a KERNEL-ENFORCED output disclaimer: every answer is framed as a statistical risk indicator, never a tax determination. The AI Communications/Customer Engagement Agent is deliberately NOT in this version: Illinois two-party consent for call recording is an unresolved legal prerequisite (MagCPA Requirement 8) — it ships as a package update once counsel confirms the consent mechanism.
name: AI CPA Firm

entities.description: One staff member's period summary from the AGORZ Time & Billing system: billable vs available hours, billed amounts, standard rate.
One location's period roll-up from AGORZ (+ QuickBooks revenue): WIP aging, billing cycle, revenue, headcount, retention, CPE-compliant staff count (fed from the person-level compliance summary).
Ratio-relevant essentials of a client business return, entered per engagement for comparison against IRS segment ratios. A risk INDICATOR input - never a determination.

capabilities.name: CPA Firm Intelligence
capabilities.description: Per-staff and per-location professional-services KPIs with deviation monitoring.

capabilities.name: Tax Risk Indication
capabilities.description: Client return ratios vs IRS segment benchmarks - statistical indicators reviewed by licensed CPAs, never determinations.

schemaTemplates.name: AGORZ staff period summary (JSON) - reference until AGORZ connection details arrive
AGORZ location period roll-up (JSON) - reference until AGORZ connection details arrive
Client return summary (manual entry per engagement)

performanceFrameworks.name: CPA Firm Operations KPI Framework v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Per-staff and per-location CPA firm KPIs (MagCPA Requirement 6), built on AGORZ Time & Billing data. Targets are AI-seeded professional-services baselines pending partner review.

okrs.objective: Healthy leverage and cash discipline at every office

performanceFrameworks.name: Client Tax Risk Indicators v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Ratio indicators per client return (MagCPA Requirement 5/7). Targets are intentionally null: expectations come from IRS corporate tax ratios BY INDUSTRY SEGMENT, set as tenant benchmarks (AI Analytical Source) - not one-size-fits-all manifest targets. Every flag is a statistical indicator a licensed CPA reviews.

agents.name: Tax Preparation & Compliance Agent
agents.description: Flags audit-risk indicators on client returns by comparing ratios against IRS segment benchmarks, citing the effective-dated reference library. Advisory only.
agents.persona: You are the Tax Preparation & Compliance Agent for a licensed CPA firm. You compare client return ratios (officers compensation/revenue, gross margin, net margin) against IRS corporate tax ratio benchmarks for the client's industry segment, and you cite the applicable reference (IRS guideline, GAAP/GAAS section, IDOR bulletin) from the reference library when explaining any flag - including which edition/effective period you relied on. Ground every statement in the provided context; if the reference library or benchmarks lack the segment in question, say so plainly. You NEVER conclude that a return is wrong, fraudulent, or non-compliant: you surface statistical deviations for a licensed CPA to evaluate. Phrase every flag as an indicator (e.g. 'deviates from the IRS segment median, which correlates with elevated audit selection risk'), never as a determination.
sampleQuestions: Which client returns deviate most from their IRS segment ratios this year?
Why was client C-1042's officers compensation ratio flagged, and which IRS guidance applies?
Which edition of the IRS guidelines applies to a tax year 2025 return?
agents.outputDisclaimer: This is a statistical risk indicator generated from IRS benchmark ratios - it is NOT a tax determination, audit prediction, or professional advice. A licensed CPA at MagCPA LLC must evaluate every flag before any client communication or filing decision.
